name: Chandrayaan-3
description: India's third lunar mission achieved a historic soft landing near the Moon's south pole.

objectives.title: Soft landing on the Moon
objectives.info: Demonstrate autonomous precision landing technology near the lunar south pole.

objectives.title: Deploy Pragyan rover
objectives.info: Validate rover deployment and surface mobility on the Moon.

objectives.title: Perform in-situ experiments
objectives.info: Collect scientific measurements directly from the lunar surface.

achievements.title: Historic lunar south pole landing
achievements.info: Became the first nation to achieve a successful soft landing near the Moon's south polar region.

achievements.title: Fourth nation to soft-land on the Moon
achievements.info: India joined the United States, Soviet Union (Russia), and China as the fourth country to successfully soft-land a spacecraft on the Moon.

achievements.title: Successful Pragyan rover deployment
achievements.info: The Pragyan rover rolled out from the Vikram lander and conducted in-situ scientific experiments on the lunar surface.

achievements.title: Validated critical landing technologies
achievements.info: Successfully demonstrated autonomous guidance, navigation, hazard detection, and controlled descent technologies for future lunar missions.

payloads.title: ChaSTE
payloads.info: Measured the thermal properties of lunar soil.

payloads.title: ILSA
payloads.info: Detected seismic activity and vibrations on the Moon.

payloads.title: RAMBHA-LP
payloads.info: Measured plasma density near the lunar surface.

engineering_significance: Validated autonomous precision landing, hazard detection, guidance algorithms, and propulsion technologies required for future lunar and planetary missions.

timeline.event: Launch aboard LVM3 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre
Historic soft landing near the Moon's south pole
Pragyan rover deployed and began scientific experiments

name: Mangalyaan
description: India became the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit and the first country to succeed on its first attempt.

objectives.title: Demonstrate interplanetary mission capability
objectives.info: Validate India's ability to design, launch and operate a spacecraft on an interplanetary mission.

objectives.title: Study the Martian surface and atmosphere
objectives.info: Collect scientific data on Mars' surface features, atmosphere and mineral composition.

objectives.title: Develop deep-space communication
objectives.info: Test navigation, tracking and communication technologies over interplanetary distances.

achievements.title: First Asian nation to reach Mars
achievements.info: India became the first Asian country to successfully place a spacecraft in Mars orbit.

achievements.title: Succeeded on the first attempt
achievements.info: India became the first nation to reach Mars orbit on its maiden mission.

achievements.title: Highly cost-effective mission
achievements.info: Completed one of the world's lowest-cost interplanetary missions.

achievements.title: Exceeded planned mission life
achievements.info: Operated for several years beyond its original six-month design life.

payloads.title: Mars Colour Camera (MCC)
payloads.info: Captured high-resolution images of Mars and its moons.

payloads.title: Methane Sensor for Mars (MSM)
payloads.info: Searched for traces of methane, a possible indicator of geological or biological activity.

payloads.title: Lyman Alpha Photometer (LAP)
payloads.info: Studied hydrogen and deuterium in the Martian atmosphere.

payloads.title: Thermal Infrared Imaging Spectrometer (TIS)
payloads.info: Mapped the mineral composition and surface temperature of Mars.

payloads.title: MENCA
payloads.info: Measured the composition of the Martian exosphere.

engineering_significance: Demonstrated India's ability to design, navigate, and operate an interplanetary spacecraft while achieving exceptional cost efficiency and reliability.

timeline.event: Conducted scientific observations and exceeded planned mission life

name: Aditya-L1
description: India's first dedicated mission to study the Sun.

objectives.title: Study the Sun's corona
objectives.info: Observe the outermost layer of the Sun to understand coronal heating.

objectives.title: Monitor solar activity
objectives.info: Study solar flares, coronal mass ejections and magnetic fields.

objectives.title: Improve space weather prediction
objectives.info: Provide data to better understand solar events affecting Earth and satellites.

achievements.title: India's first solar observatory
achievements.info: The country's first dedicated mission to study the Sun.

achievements.title: Reached the L1 point
achievements.info: Successfully entered a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point.

achievements.title: Continuous solar observation
achievements.info: Provides uninterrupted observations of the Sun without Earth's eclipses.

payloads.title: VELC
payloads.info: Observes the solar corona in visible and infrared wavelengths.

payloads.title: SUIT
payloads.info: Captures ultraviolet images of the Sun's photosphere and chromosphere.

payloads.title: SoLEXS
payloads.info: Measures low-energy solar X-ray emissions.

payloads.title: HEL1OS
payloads.info: Observes high-energy solar X-rays.

payloads.title: ASPEX
payloads.info: Studies particles in the solar wind.

payloads.title: PAPA
payloads.info: Measures the composition of solar wind plasma.

payloads.title: Magnetometer
payloads.info: Measures magnetic fields at the L1 Lagrange point.

engineering_significance: Established India's capability to continuously observe the Sun from the L1 Lagrange point, improving space-weather forecasting and advancing heliophysics research.

timeline.event: Successfully inserted into halo orbit around the L1 point
Continuous observation of the Sun and collection of scientific data

name: Gaganyaan

objectives.title: Demonstrate human spaceflight capability
objectives.info: Show India's ability to independently send astronauts into space.

objectives.title: Develop indigenous crew systems
objectives.info: Build crew modules, life-support systems and recovery technologies within India.

objectives.title: Ensure astronaut safety
objectives.info: Validate emergency escape, re-entry and recovery systems.

achievements.title: Crew Escape Test successful
achievements.info: Validated the emergency escape system through a successful test flight.

achievements.title: Major indigenous technologies developed
achievements.info: Built key human-spaceflight technologies within India.

achievements.title: Foundation for future crewed missions
achievements.info: Establishes capabilities for long-term human space exploration.

payloads.title: Crew Module
payloads.info: Pressurized spacecraft that carries astronauts during the mission.

payloads.title: Service Module
payloads.info: Provides propulsion, electrical power and mission support.

payloads.title: Environmental Control & Life Support System
payloads.info: Maintains breathable air, temperature and pressure for the crew.

payloads.title: Crew Escape System
payloads.info: Rapidly separates the crew module from the rocket during emergencies.

payloads.title: Human-rated LVM3
payloads.info: A modified LVM3 launch vehicle certified for carrying astronauts.

engineering_significance: Developing indigenous human spaceflight technologies—including crew safety, life support, and human-rated launch systems—lays the foundation for India's future crewed space missions.

timeline.event: Crew Escape System Test Vehicle mission successfully completed
Uncrewed orbital test missions and system validation
First crewed Gaganyaan mission to Low Earth Orbit
Recovery of astronauts after a safe splashdown in the Indian Ocean

name: AstroSat
description: India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory designed to study celestial objects across ultraviolet and X-ray wavelengths.
engineering_significance: Established India's capability to build and operate a space observatory with multiple scientific instruments observing the universe simultaneously.

objectives.title: Study compact stars
objectives.info: Observe neutron stars, pulsars and black holes in multiple wavelengths.

objectives.title: Observe high-energy universe
objectives.info: Study energetic cosmic phenomena such as quasars and X-ray binaries.

objectives.title: Multi-wavelength astronomy
objectives.info: Observe celestial objects simultaneously in ultraviolet and X-ray bands.

payloads.title: UVIT
payloads.info: Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope captures high-resolution ultraviolet images.

payloads.title: LAXPC
payloads.info: Large Area X-ray Proportional Counter studies X-ray emissions from compact objects.

payloads.title: CZTI
payloads.info: Cadmium Zinc Telluride Imager observes hard X-rays and gamma-ray bursts.

payloads.title: SXT
payloads.info: Soft X-ray Telescope studies low-energy X-ray sources.

payloads.title: SSM
payloads.info: Scanning Sky Monitor detects transient X-ray events.

achievements.title: India's first space observatory
achievements.info: Marked India's entry into dedicated space-based astronomy.

achievements.title: International scientific collaboration
achievements.info: Used by researchers worldwide for astrophysical observations.

achievements.title: Long operational life
achievements.info: Continues producing valuable scientific data well beyond its planned lifetime.

name: XPoSat
description: India's first dedicated X-ray Polarimeter Satellite, designed to study the polarization of cosmic X-rays from black holes, neutron stars and other energetic celestial objects.
engineering_significance: Introduced India's capability to perform space-based X-ray polarimetry, helping scientists understand the geometry and emission mechanisms of extreme cosmic environments.

objectives.title: Study X-ray polarization
objectives.info: Measure the polarization of X-rays emitted by celestial sources.

objectives.title: Observe compact objects
objectives.info: Investigate black holes, neutron stars and pulsars.

objectives.title: Advance high-energy astrophysics
objectives.info: Provide data that complements traditional X-ray observations.

payloads.title: POLIX
payloads.info: Measures the polarization of medium-energy X-rays from astronomical sources.

payloads.title: XSPECT
payloads.info: Analyzes the energy spectrum and timing characteristics of X-ray sources.

achievements.title: India's first X-ray polarimetry mission
achievements.info: Established India's capability in space-based X-ray polarization studies.

achievements.title: Successful deployment
achievements.info: Successfully launched and commissioned aboard PSLV-C58.

achievements.title: Supports global astrophysics research
achievements.info: Provides valuable scientific observations of high-energy cosmic phenomena.

name: NISAR
description: NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) is a joint Earth observation satellite designed to monitor changes in Earth's land and ice surfaces with unprecedented precision.
engineering_significance: Demonstrates advanced dual-frequency radar imaging and international collaboration between ISRO and NASA.

objectives.title: Monitor Earth's surface
objectives.info: Measure changes in forests, glaciers, volcanoes and earthquakes.

objectives.title: Support disaster management
objectives.info: Provide rapid data for floods, landslides and natural disasters.

objectives.title: Climate monitoring
objectives.info: Study ice sheets, biomass and land deformation.

payloads.title: L-band SAR
payloads.info: NASA-built radar capable of penetrating vegetation.

payloads.title: S-band SAR
payloads.info: ISRO-built radar for high-resolution Earth observation.

achievements.title: NASA-ISRO collaboration
achievements.info: One of the largest Earth observation collaborations between the two agencies.

achievements.title: Advanced radar technology
achievements.info: Combines L-band and S-band SAR in a single satellite.

name: Cartosat Series
description: The Cartosat series provides high-resolution images for mapping, infrastructure planning, urban development and national security.
engineering_significance: Provides indigenous high-resolution satellite imaging for mapping and strategic applications.

objectives.title: High-resolution mapping
objectives.info: Generate detailed maps of India's terrain.

objectives.title: Urban planning
objectives.info: Support smart cities and infrastructure development.

objectives.title: National security
objectives.info: Provide strategic imagery for defence applications.

payloads.title: Panchromatic Camera
payloads.info: Captures very high-resolution black-and-white images.

payloads.title: Multispectral Camera
payloads.info: Captures images in multiple spectral bands.

achievements.title: Supports Digital India
achievements.info: Provides mapping data for infrastructure projects.

achievements.title: Strategic imaging
achievements.info: One of India's most capable Earth imaging satellite families.

timeline.event: Successive Cartosat satellites expanded imaging capability.

name: RISAT Series
description: RISAT satellites use Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) to observe Earth day and night, regardless of weather conditions.
engineering_significance: Introduced all-weather radar imaging capability for agriculture, disaster management and national security.

objectives.title: All-weather observation
objectives.info: Capture images through clouds and during nighttime.

objectives.title: Agricultural monitoring
objectives.info: Assess crops and soil moisture.

objectives.title: Disaster response
objectives.info: Support flood and cyclone monitoring.

payloads.title: Synthetic Aperture Radar
payloads.info: Produces high-resolution radar images independent of weather.

achievements.title: First indigenous radar imaging capability
achievements.info: Enabled continuous Earth observation in all weather conditions.

achievements.title: Supports disaster management
achievements.info: Provides critical imagery during natural disasters.

timeline.event: RISAT satellites continue Earth observation missions.

name: Oceansat & Scatsat
description: The Oceansat and Scatsat satellite series monitor Earth's oceans, weather patterns, wind speeds and marine ecosystems to support fisheries, climate research and cyclone forecasting.
engineering_significance: Provides continuous oceanographic and atmospheric observations that support weather forecasting, marine resource management and disaster preparedness.

objectives.title: Monitor ocean colour
objectives.info: Study chlorophyll concentration and marine biological productivity.

objectives.title: Measure ocean surface winds
objectives.info: Provide accurate wind vector data for weather prediction.

objectives.title: Support fisheries
objectives.info: Help identify potential fishing zones using satellite observations.

payloads.title: Ocean Colour Monitor (OCM)
payloads.info: Measures ocean colour to estimate biological activity.

payloads.title: Scatterometer
payloads.info: Measures ocean surface wind speed and direction.

achievements.title: Improved cyclone forecasting
achievements.info: Provided valuable ocean wind data for weather prediction.

achievements.title: Supports Indian fisheries
achievements.info: Helps identify Potential Fishing Zones (PFZs).

achievements.title: Climate monitoring
achievements.info: Contributes to long-term ocean and climate studies.

timeline.event: Scatsat-1 launched for wind vector observations.
Operational support for weather and ocean monitoring.

name: INSAT & GSAT
description: The INSAT and GSAT satellite series provide communication, television broadcasting, weather services and broadband connectivity across India.
engineering_significance: Established India's indigenous communication satellite infrastructure supporting telecommunications, broadcasting and meteorology.

objectives.title: Telecommunication
objectives.info: Provide reliable communication services across the country.

objectives.title: Television broadcasting
objectives.info: Support satellite television and educational broadcasting.

objectives.title: Meteorological observations
objectives.info: Monitor weather systems and improve forecasting.

payloads.title: Communication Transponders
payloads.info: Relay television, voice and broadband communication.

payloads.title: Meteorological Sensors
payloads.info: Monitor cloud cover and weather systems.

achievements.title: Nationwide communication network
achievements.info: Connected remote regions through satellite communication.

achievements.title: Supports disaster communication
achievements.info: Provides communication services during emergencies.

achievements.title: Strengthened Digital India
achievements.info: Supports television, internet and educational services.

timeline.event: Successive GSAT satellites enhanced communication services.

name: NavIC
description: NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) is India's independent regional satellite navigation system providing accurate positioning, navigation and timing services.
engineering_significance: Provides India with an independent satellite navigation capability, reducing dependence on foreign navigation systems.

objectives.title: Regional navigation
objectives.info: Provide accurate positioning services across India and nearby regions.

objectives.title: Timing services
objectives.info: Deliver precise timing for critical infrastructure.

objectives.title: Strategic independence
objectives.info: Ensure reliable navigation services without relying on foreign systems.

payloads.title: Navigation Payload
payloads.info: Transmits navigation signals for positioning services.

payloads.title: Ranging Payload
payloads.info: Supports accurate distance measurements between satellites and ground stations.

achievements.title: India's own navigation system
achievements.info: Established an independent regional navigation satellite constellation.

achievements.title: Supports civilian and strategic users
achievements.info: Provides positioning services for transportation, disaster response and defence.

achievements.title: Growing device integration
achievements.info: Increasing adoption in smartphones, vehicles and navigation devices.

timeline.event: Navigation services expanded across multiple sectors.

name: Aryabhata
description: Aryabhata was India's first satellite, marking the beginning of the country's space age.It demonstrated India's capability in designing and building satellites.
engineering_significance: Laid the foundation for India's indigenous satellite programme and inspired decades of advancements in space technology.

objectives.title: Demonstrate satellite technology
objectives.info: Validate India's ability to design, build and operate an artificial satellite.

objectives.title: Conduct scientific experiments
objectives.info: Perform studies in X-ray astronomy, aeronomy and solar physics.

objectives.title: Build indigenous expertise
objectives.info: Develop engineering experience for future Indian satellites.

payloads.title: X-ray Astronomy Experiment
payloads.info: Studied cosmic X-ray emissions.

payloads.title: Solar Physics Experiment
payloads.info: Observed solar radiation and related phenomena.

payloads.title: Aeronomy Experiment
payloads.info: Collected data about Earth's upper atmosphere.

achievements.title: India's first satellite
achievements.info: Marked the birth of India's satellite programme.

achievements.title: Foundation for ISRO missions
achievements.info: Provided valuable engineering knowledge for future missions.

achievements.title: Historic milestone
achievements.info: Established India as a spacefaring nation.

timeline.event: Successfully launched from Kapustin Yar, USSR.
Remembered as the beginning of India's satellite journey.
